nodes.name: Update Last Sale Date & Days No Sale

parameters.content:
AUTOMATED PRICING SYSTEM — OVERVIEW

Purpose: Automatically manages prices for slow-selling shoes

How it works:
• Runs daily at 2:00 AM (Berlin time)
• Only affects shoes with Tag 16 or Tag 17
• Reduces price by €1 every 3 days with no sales
• Increases price by €2 when a shoe sells
• Never goes below a calculated minimum price
• Resets to original price after 7 days at minimum (adds Tag 15)

Logs everything to Google Sheets for complete audit trail.

NOTE: Change manual trigger to cron trigger for production.

STEP 1: LOGIN & FETCH DATA

This section:
1. Logs into PlentyOne automatically
2. Fetches all shoes with Tag 16 and Tag 17
3. Fetches all orders from the last 24 hours

What to check:
• All nodes should show green checkmarks
• If red, check credentials or API connection
• Tag 16 and Tag 17 streams run simultaneously

TAG 16 & TAG 17 STREAMS

Tag 16 and Tag 17 have different minimum price formulas:
• Tag 16: ((EK + 3.84) / 0.81 / 0.89) + 8.96
• Tag 17: ((EK + 0.84) / 0.81 / 0.89) + 8.96

EK = purchase price (from PlentyOne)

These formulas are applied automatically in the "floorPrice" calculation nodes.

POSITIVE STOCK FILTER

Only shoes with stock > 0 are processed.
Why? No need to price shoes we don't have.

If a shoe sells and stock becomes 0:
• It will not appear in pricing decisions
• Will reappear automatically when restocked
• Sale is still recorded in Google Sheets state

STATE SHEET — REMEMBERING EACH SHOE

The State sheet (Google Sheets) tracks:
• Current stage (Uninitiated, Stage 1, Stage 2, At Floor, Reset)
• Last price change date
• Last sale date
• Days at floor counter

This is the system's "memory" — without it, every day would be Day 1.

First run: The sheet will be empty. That's normal — it fills up over time.

SOLD TODAY DETECTION

This section:
1. Fetches all orders from last 24 hours
2. Extracts which shoe variations sold
3. Updates the State sheet with new sale date
4. Flags the variant as "sold today" for pricing decisions

Why this matters: Sales trigger a €2 price increase.

MERGING ALL DATA

This section combines:
• Current variant data (from PlentyOne)
• Historical state (from Google Sheets)
• Recent sales data (from orders)

Then calculates:
• Days since last price change
• Days since last sale
• Whether the shoe sold today

Result: Each shoe now has everything needed to make a pricing decision.

DAILY OPERATION

The workflow runs automatically at 2:00 AM Berlin time.

To run manually:
1. Click the "Execute Workflow" button
2. Wait for all nodes to turn green
3. Check Google Sheets to verify changes

To add new shoes to the system:
1. Add Tag 16 or Tag 17 to the shoe in PlentyOne
2. Ensure stock is positive
3. Run the workflow — it will appear automatically

What to check daily:
• Google Sheets → State tab (see current stages)
• Google Sheets → Price Change Log (verify changes)
• Google Sheets → Errors tab (resolve any issues)

DECISION ROUTER — PRICING RULES

Rules are checked in this order (first match wins):

1) SOLD TODAY → Increase price by €2 (capped at original price)

2) AT MINIMUM for 7+ days → Reset to original price + add Tag 15

3) NEVER PRICED before + 3+ days no sales → Stage 1: original price minus €1

4) ALREADY PRICED + 3+ days no sales → Stage 2: current price minus €1

5) None of the above → Skip (no change)

Rules are applied automatically based on each shoe's sales history.

EXECUTING PRICE CHANGES

Each pricing rule triggers an API call to PlentyOne:

• Sale Increase: Updates Price 17 (dynamic price)
• Reset: Restores Price 17 + adds Tag 15
• Stage 1: Creates Price 17 + assigns special UUID
• Stage 2: Updates Price 17

If any API call fails, the error is logged but other variants continue processing.

LOGGING — EVERY CHANGE TRACKED

Three Google Sheets tabs:

1. State Sheet — Current status of each shoe
   • Updated after every price change

2. Price Change Log — Complete history
   • Every price change, with old price, new price, reason, stage

3. Errors — Any failures
   • Timestamp, variant ID, action type, error message

Check these sheets to verify the system is working correctly.

assignments.name: reason
assignments.value: Sold today — price increased by €2, capped at price3

assignments.name: reason
assignments.value: 7+ days at floor with no sales — restoring price3 and adding Tag 15

assignments.name: reason
assignments.value: No sale in 3+ days — initialising price17 at price3-1 (min floor)

assignments.name: reason
assignments.value: 3+ days since last change, no sale — reducing price17 by €1
